title: Target policy distribution by audience criteria
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to distribute policies based on company, location, department, group, employment type, or individual employees using AND/OR logic, so that the right policies reach exactly the right audience.
acceptance_criteria: Given a policy to distribute, when I define the audience, then I can select company, location, department, group, employment type, and/or individual employees as scope criteria.; Given multiple criteria, when I combine them, then the system supports both AND and OR logic to build the target audience.; Given a defined scope, when I preview the distribution, then the resulting de-duplicated list of recipients is shown before sending; Given an employee matches more than one criterion, when the audience is resolved, then the employee receives the policy only once.; Given an empty or zero-match audience, when I attempt to distribute, then the system warns me that no recipients match before proceeding.

title: Multiple distribution methods
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to distribute policies manually, on a schedule, or automatically on events, so that I can match the distribution method to each situation.
acceptance_criteria: Given a policy, when I choose manual distribution, then it is sent immediately to the selected audience.; Given a policy, when I choose scheduled distribution, then it is queued and sent at the configured future date/time.; Given a scheduled distribution, when the scheduled time has not yet arrived, then I can still edit or cancel it.; Given an event trigger (e.g. onboarding, transfer, role change), when the event occurs, then the policy is distributed automatically to the affected employee(s) without manual action.; Given any distribution method, when the policy is delivered, then each recipient's assignment is timestamped and recorded for tracking.

title: Bulk policy distribution
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to distribute policies in bulk, so that I can efficiently reach large groups of employees at once.
acceptance_criteria: Given a large target audience, when I run a bulk distribution, then the policy is delivered to all matching employees in a single operation.; Given a bulk distribution, when it completes, then I can view a summary of successful and failed deliveries.; Given failed deliveries in a bulk run, when I review the summary, then I can identify and retry the failed recipients without resending to those already delivered.

title: Configure acknowledgment types
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to set an acknowledgment type of Required, Optional, or Read-Only for each policy, so that I can control the level of compliance enforcement.
acceptance_criteria: Given a policy set to Required, when it is distributed, then acknowledgment is mandatory and enforced for each recipient with due-date and escalation tracking.; Given a policy set to Optional, when it is distributed, then acknowledgment is recommended but not enforced and no overdue status is raised.; Given a policy set to Read-Only, when it is distributed, then it is presented as information only with no acknowledgment action required.; Given a policy, when I configure it, then exactly one acknowledgment type applies and the type is visible to recipients.

title: Configure acknowledgment due dates
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to configure acknowledgment due dates using fixed, relative, hire-based, or periodic renewal rules, so that deadlines fit each policy's compliance needs.
acceptance_criteria: Given a policy, when I set a fixed due date, then all recipients share that exact deadline.; Given a policy, when I set a relative due date, then the deadline is calculated as a configured number of days from the distribution date per recipient.; Given a policy, when I set a hire-based due date, then the deadline is derived from each employee's hire date.; Given a policy, when I set periodic renewal, then a new acknowledgment cycle and due date recur automatically on the configured period.; Given any due-date rule, when the deadline is computed, then the resulting due date is shown to both admin and recipient.

title: Automated reminders at SLA milestones
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to receive automated reminders as my acknowledgment deadline approaches, so that I do not miss a required sign-off.
acceptance_criteria: Given a pending acknowledgment with an SLA, when 50%, 75%, and 100% of the SLA have elapsed, then automated reminders are sent to the employee at each milestone.; Given an acknowledgment is completed, when a later reminder milestone is reached, then no further reminders are sent for that policy.; Given a Read-Only or Optional policy, when milestones elapse, then no enforcement reminders are sent.; Given a reminder is sent, when the employee opens it, then it links directly to the pending policy in their inbox.

title: Escalation for overdue acknowledgments
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want overdue acknowledgments to trigger escalation workflows based on policy criticality, so that non-compliance is surfaced and acted upon.
acceptance_criteria: Given an acknowledgment is overdue (100% of SLA passed without sign-off), when the escalation workflow runs, then the appropriate escalation path is followed based on the policy's criticality.; Given a critical policy, when it becomes overdue, then escalation is routed with higher urgency (e.g. to management) than for a non-critical policy.; Given an escalation is raised, when the employee subsequently acknowledges, then the escalation is closed automatically.; Given escalation configuration, when I set it up, then it uses the workflow engine to determine approvers/notified parties.

title: Trigger re-acknowledgment
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the system to require re-acknowledgment when relevant changes occur, so that employees always confirm the current version of the policy.
acceptance_criteria: Given a policy's content changes, when the new version is published, then affected employees are required to re-acknowledge the updated policy.; Given a periodic renewal expires, when the renewal date passes, then re-acknowledgment is required for the recurring cycle.; Given an employee transfers, when the transfer is recorded, then re-acknowledgment is triggered for the policies relevant to their new placement.; Given an employee changes role, when the change is recorded, then re-acknowledgment is triggered for role-relevant policies.; Given a regulatory update, when it is applied to a policy, then affected employees are required to re-acknowledge.; Given re-acknowledgment is triggered, when it recurs, then the prior acknowledgment record is preserved in the audit trail and a new pending item is created.

title: Employee policy inbox and review
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want a policy inbox showing my pending acknowledgments with a review interface, so that I can read and act on policies assigned to me.
acceptance_criteria: Given policies assigned to me, when I open my policy inbox, then I see all pending acknowledgments with their acknowledgment type and due date.; Given a pending policy, when I open it, then I can review its full content in the policy review interface before acknowledging.; Given a Read-Only policy, when I open it, then it is shown for information with no acknowledgment button.; Given items in my inbox, when I view them, then required and overdue items are clearly distinguished from optional ones.

title: Acknowledgment confirmation with receipt
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to confirm my acknowledgment and receive a receipt, so that I have proof I completed the required sign-off.
acceptance_criteria: Given a policy I have reviewed, when I confirm acknowledgment, then the confirmation is recorded with timestamp and a receipt is generated for me.; Given I have acknowledged a policy, when I revisit my inbox, then the policy no longer appears as pending.; Given I have acknowledged a policy, when I want proof later, then I can retrieve or download the acknowledgment receipt.; Given a required policy, when I have not confirmed, then I cannot mark it complete without an explicit confirmation action.

title: Compliance reporting and audit trail
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want acknowledgment status, pending/overdue reports, and a compliance dashboard with long-term audit retention, so that I can monitor and evidence policy compliance.
acceptance_criteria: Given distributed policies, when I open acknowledgment reporting, then I can view acknowledgment status reports showing acknowledged, pending, and overdue counts per policy and per employee.; Given reporting, when I filter, then I can produce dedicated pending and overdue reports.; Given the compliance dashboard, when I view it, then it summarizes overall acknowledgment compliance across policies and audiences.; Given an acknowledgment event, when it is recorded, then it is retained in the audit trail for 7 years.; Given the audit trail, when I inspect a record, then it shows who acknowledged, when, and which policy version.

title: Integration with lifecycle, workflow, and tasks
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want policy distribution to integrate with employee lifecycle, the workflow engine, and the task/checklist module, so that acknowledgments are automated and coordinated with other processes.
acceptance_criteria: Given an onboarding or transfer event, when it occurs, then the employee lifecycle integration triggers the relevant policy distribution automatically.; Given an overdue acknowledgment, when escalation is needed, then the workflow engine executes the configured escalation.; Given a distributed policy, when acknowledgment is pending, then a corresponding item appears in the task/checklist module for the employee.; Given an acknowledgment is completed, when the receipt is recorded, then the linked task/checklist item is marked complete.

title: Distribute policies across companies in a group
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want to distribute policies and monitor acknowledgment across the multiple companies in my group, so that shared or regulatory policies are enforced consistently group-wide.
acceptance_criteria: Given multiple companies under my group, when I define a distribution scope, then I can select one, several, or all companies in the group plus location/department/group/employment-type/individual criteria using AND/OR logic.; Given a group-wide distribution, when it runs, then each company's employees receive the policy while respecting company-level configuration.; Given group distributions, when I open reporting, then I can view acknowledgment status, pending, and overdue rolled up per company and consolidated across the group.
source: FR 6.11.1 / FR 6.11.8 (Distribution Scope across companies; consolidated reporting)

title: Portfolio-wide policy compliance oversight
user_story: As a Portfolio Admin, I want a portfolio-level compliance dashboard and audit visibility across all group companies in my portfolio, so that I can evidence and enforce policy compliance at the highest tenant level.
acceptance_criteria: Given all group companies in my portfolio, when I open the compliance dashboard, then it summarizes acknowledgment compliance aggregated across the entire portfolio with drill-down to group and company level.; Given overdue or non-compliant policies, when I review the portfolio dashboard, then I can identify which companies and critical policies are at risk.; Given an acknowledgment event anywhere in the portfolio, when I inspect the audit trail, then records are retained for 7 years and accessible for audit and regulatory evidence.
source: FR 6.11.8 (Reporting — portfolio-level compliance dashboard and audit)

title: Read-only distribution and proxy acknowledgment for non-users
user_story: As an Employee (Non-User) without self-service access, I want policies distributed to me as read-only or acknowledged on my behalf by an admin, so that non-portal staff are still covered by policy compliance.
acceptance_criteria: Given a policy is distributed to an audience that includes non-users, when the audience is resolved, then non-user employees are included and flagged as unable to self-acknowledge.; Given a required policy for a non-user, when the employee reviews it offline, then a Company Admin can record acknowledgment on their behalf with the confirming actor, date, and evidence captured in the audit trail.; Given a Read-Only policy for a non-user, when it is distributed, then it is recorded as delivered with no acknowledgment expected; Given proxy acknowledgment is recorded, when reporting runs, then the non-user's status reflects compliance consistently with self-service acknowledgments.
source: FR 6.11.3 / FR 6.11.7 (Acknowledgment Types; Self-Service coverage for non-users)

title: Configure reminder SLA thresholds and escalation policies
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want to configure the reminder milestones and escalation workflows tied to policy criticality, so that reminder and escalation behavior is standardized and enforceable across the platform.
acceptance_criteria: Given reminder configuration, when I set it up, then reminders default to 50%, 75%, and 100% of the SLA and can be adjusted per criticality where permitted.; Given escalation configuration, when I define escalation paths, then each path is mapped to a policy criticality level and executed via the workflow engine on overdue acknowledgments.; Given a criticality-to-escalation mapping, when a policy is created, then the applicable reminder and escalation behavior is inherited automatically.

title: Bitemporal acknowledgment data model with tenant isolation
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want acknowledgment assignments and events persisted in a tenant-scoped bitemporal data model, so that every acknowledgment is uniquely tracked, historically reconstructable, and isolated per tenant.
acceptance_criteria: Given a policy is distributed, when an assignment is created, then a tenant-scoped acknowledgment record is persisted linking employee, policy, and specific policy version, enforced by row-level security so no tenant can read another tenant's records.; Given an employee-policy-version pair, when acknowledgment is recorded, then a uniqueness constraint prevents duplicate active acknowledgments for the same version.; Given an acknowledgment progresses through assigned, pending, acknowledged, or overdue states, when each transition occurs, then it is stored with effective-dated (valid-time) and system-recorded (transaction-time) history so prior states are never overwritten.; Given a re-acknowledgment supersedes a prior one, when the new record is created, then the earlier acknowledgment remains queryable as history rather than being deleted.; Given audit or regulatory retention, when records age, then they are retained for 7 years and remain reconstructable as-of any past date.
source: FR 6.11.6, FR 6.11.8 (L1 data model, bitemporal history, RLS, retention)

title: Governed notification and receipt templates
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want reminder, escalation, and acknowledgment-receipt content driven by per-tenant governed templates, so that messaging is consistent, branded, and changeable without code.
acceptance_criteria: Given the notification/template config, when I edit a reminder or escalation template, then the change is captured as a new versioned, effective-dated template that the notification engine reads at send time.; Given an acknowledgment receipt, when it is generated, then its layout and wording come from a governed receipt template rather than hard-coded text.; Given a template supports variables, when a reminder is rendered, then employee name, policy title, due date, and inbox link are merged from the assignment record.; Given a template is updated, when in-flight assignments exist, then the effective-dated version applicable at send time is used and prior versions remain on record.
source: FR 6.11.5, FR 6.11.7 (L2 config — notification and receipt templates)

title: Re-acknowledgment trigger decision table
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want the events that force re-acknowledgment defined in a versioned decision table, so that compliance rules can be governed and changed without code.
acceptance_criteria: Given re-acknowledgment configuration, when I define triggers, then content change, periodic renewal expiry, transfer, role change, and regulatory update are expressed as decision-table rows that the rules engine evaluates.; Given the decision table, when I add, disable, or reorder a trigger condition, then the change is versioned and effective-dated without a deployment.; Given a trigger maps to a scope, when it fires, then the table specifies which policies and which affected employees require re-acknowledgment.; Given a decision-table change, when it takes effect, then prior evaluations remain reproducible against the version that was active at the time.

title: Engine-driven audience resolution and event-triggered distribution
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the rules and workflow engines to resolve audiences and execute event-triggered distributions, so that targeting and automation behave consistently from shared runtime logic.
acceptance_criteria: Given AND/OR scope criteria, when a distribution is submitted, then the rules engine evaluates the criteria against current employee data to produce a de-duplicated recipient set.; Given a lifecycle event (onboarding, transfer, role change), when it occurs, then the engine matches configured triggers and the workflow engine executes the corresponding distribution without manual action.; Given an overdue acknowledgment, when the SLA passes, then the workflow engine runs the criticality-mapped escalation path defined in config.; Given employee data changes after resolution, when a distribution re-runs, then the engine re-evaluates membership so newly matching employees are included and no-longer-matching ones are handled per configuration.
source: FR 6.11.1, FR 6.11.2, FR 6.11.5 (L3 engines — rules and workflow evaluation)

title: Distribution management console and status grid
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want a metadata-driven console listing distributions and per-recipient acknowledgment status with search and filtering, so that I can manage and monitor distributions from the UI.
acceptance_criteria: Given active and past distributions, when I open the management console, then a grid lists each distribution with audience, method, acknowledgment type, due date, and aggregate status.; Given a distribution, when I drill into it, then I see per-recipient status (pending, acknowledged, overdue) that I can search and filter by employee, department, or status.; Given the grid, when I filter or sort, then results update without a page reload and can be exported.; Given a pending distribution, when I select it, then I can edit, cancel, or retry failed recipients directly from the console.
source: FR 6.11.7, FR 6.11.8 (L4 presentation — metadata-driven admin console/grid)
